seo_title: GEM: Geometric Entropy Mixing for Optimal LLM Data Curation
meta_description: Discover how GEM (Geometric Entropy Mixing) improves LLM data curation and boosts downstream accuracy by up to 1.2%.
body_html:
Direct answer: GEM (Geometric Entropy Mixing) is a framework that reformulates data curation as a variational problem on the hypersphere augmented with a mixing-balance regularizer.

GEM improves average downstream accuracy by up to 1.2% when integrated into mixing strategies like DoReMi and RegMix. The framework effectively counteracts cluster collapse to discover balanced semantic structures invisible to Euclidean heuristics. Experiments with 1.1B-parameter models demonstrate the effectiveness of GEM in boosting downstream accuracy.

GEM is scaled to web-scale corpora using teacher-student distillation, enabling robust coordinate systems for predictable data mixing. The framework introduces the Geometric Influence Score (GIS) for interpretable taxonomy generation. Properly filtered and deduplicated web data alone can lead to powerful models, even outperforming curated corpora.

GEM establishes a new state-of-the-art in LLM data curation. The framework offers a robust coordinate system for predictable data mixing. GEM effectively counteracts cluster collapse to discover balanced semantic structures.

source.title: GEM: Geometric Entropy Mixing for Optimal LLM Data Curation
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new framework called Geometric Entropy Mixing (GEM) to improve the curation of large language model (LLM) training datasets. GEM addresses issues with existing methods, such as Euclidean clustering, by using a variational problem on the hypersphere and a mixing-balance regularizer. The framework has been shown to outperform existing methods in improving downstream accuracy, with average gains of up to 1.2% when integrated into popular mixing strategies like DoReMi and RegMix.
